title: Planet Labs PBC (PL) Q4 Fiscal 2026 Earnings Recap
text:
Planet Labs PBC delivered Q4 fiscal 2026 earnings results on March 19, 2026, marking significant achievements in profitability and backlog expansion. The company reported record quarterly revenue of $86.8 million, exceeding analyst expectations of $77.81 million by 11.55%. The quarterly result represented 41% year-over-year growth, demonstrating accelerating momentum.

Full-year fiscal 2026 revenue reached $307.7 million, representing 26% year-over-year growth and marking the company's continued trajectory toward market leadership in Earth observation. The company achieved its long-awaited first full year of non-GAAP profitability with adjusted EBITDA of $15.5 million, compared to losses of $11 million in the prior year.

Free cash flow performance showed dramatic improvement, reaching positive $52.9 million for fiscal 2026 compared to negative $64 million in the previous year. This transformation demonstrates the company's ability to convert revenue growth into operating cash flow, providing capital for growth investments. The company's cash position strengthened to $640.1 million, providing substantial financial flexibility.

Backlog surged 79% year-over-year to reach $900 million, with remaining performance obligations up 106% to $852 million. This backlog represents approximately three years of revenues at current run-rates, providing substantial visibility. The Defense & Intelligence segment showed the strongest growth, expanding over 50% year-over-year, driven by major government contracts.

For fiscal 2027, Planet projects revenue of $415-440 million, representing 35-43% growth at the midpoint. This guidance reflects confidence in pulling forward backlog while maintaining profitability as the company invests in satellite deployment and AI solution development. The earnings recap validates Planet Labs' transformation into a profitable, growth-oriented space technology company.
